description: LOCK detection rules for IIS behavioral analysis — 17 classes covering all major web attack TTPs

classes.name: Injection: SQL Injection
classes.retrieval_strategy: Count HTTP 500 errors from IIS logs in the window. Drill down on new source IPs hitting new URI paths. Server errors from novel sources on novel endpoints suggest injection probing.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this Status:500 pattern on new URIs from this IP suggest SQL injection probing?
benign_exceptions: Known application bugs that produce 500 on specific endpoints
Load balancer health-check mismatches
Nessus scanner IPs in allow_listed_ips

classes.name: Injection: Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate POST requests to IIS. Drill on new source IPs sending POSTs with unusually long query strings (>200 chars). Long query strings from new IPs to form endpoints suggest XSS payload injection.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this POST with long query string to a form endpoint suggest XSS payload injection?
benign_exceptions: File upload endpoints that accept large payloads
API endpoints with JWT tokens in body
Known rich-text editors submitting HTML content

classes.name: Injection: Command / Remote Code Execution
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate HTTP 200s with Time_Taken >5000ms. High latency successful responses from new IPs on new URIs suggest sleep-based command injection or OAST callbacks. Drill to identify pattern.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this high-latency 200 from a new IP on a new URI suggest sleep-based command injection?
benign_exceptions: Report generation endpoints legitimately taking >5s
Search endpoints with complex queries
Known batch processing URIs

classes.name: Path Traversal / Local File Inclusion
classes.retrieval_strategy: Search for URI paths containing '..' sequences in IIS logs. Any dot-dot traversal in a URI is anomalous regardless of source IP status. Drill to confirm scope and target files.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this URI path pattern suggest directory traversal or local file inclusion?
benign_exceptions: No known benign exceptions — dot-dot in URI should always be reviewed

classes.name: SSRF / Cloud Metadata Probe
classes.retrieval_strategy: Search URI_Query fields for internal IP ranges (169.254.x.x, 127.0.0.x) or localhost. SSRF patterns in query strings are never benign and should always be escalated. Always force REVIEW.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this request contain SSRF payloads targeting internal IPs or cloud metadata?
benign_exceptions: No known benign exceptions — SSRF payloads should always be reviewed

classes.name: Exploit Payloads: Log4Shell / SSTI / XXE
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate all IIS traffic, then filter for oversized query strings (>300 chars) from new source IPs. Extreme query string length combined with new source IP suggests exploit payload delivery such as Log4Shell JNDI strings, SSTI templates, or XXE entities.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this oversized query string suggest exploit payload delivery (Log4Shell, SSTI, XXE)?
benign_exceptions: Known search endpoints accepting complex filters
API endpoints with base64-encoded payloads
OAuth/SAML assertion endpoints

classes.name: Webshell Behavior
classes.retrieval_strategy: Two sub-checks: (7a) POST requests returning HTTP 200 to URIs new in registry suggest webshell command execution. (7b) POST requests returning 200 to static file extensions (jpg/png/css/js/gif) are always suspicious regardless of IP status — static assets do not accept POST.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this POST to a new URI or static extension suggest a webshell executing commands?
benign_exceptions: CDN origin callback URIs that may have .js extension
Known image upload endpoints (7a only, not 7b)

classes.name: Authentication Attacks: 401-Storm
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate HTTP 401 responses in a 15-minute window, then group by source IP. High 401 rates per IP suggest credential stuffing or password spraying. Also check for 200 following 401 pattern from same IP which indicates successful compromise after brute force.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this IP's 401 rate and target distribution suggest credential stuffing or password spraying?
benign_exceptions: Monitoring agents with stale credentials being refreshed
Known IP ranges rotating API keys
Service accounts after password rotation

classes.name: Enumeration / 404 Sweep
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate HTTP 404 responses in 1-hour window. Count unique URI paths per source IP. High breadth of unique 404 URIs from a single IP suggests systematic endpoint enumeration — attackers mapping the application surface before targeted exploitation.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this breadth of unique 404 URIs suggest systematic endpoint enumeration?
benign_exceptions: Link-checking crawlers from known marketing platforms
SEO monitoring tools on allow list
Internal health check misconfiguration

classes.name: Scanner / Automation User-Agent
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate User-Agent distribution across IIS traffic. New User-Agents not in entity registry combined with high request volume (>1000 in window) suggest automated scanner activity. Well-known scanner UA strings should auto-promote to REVIEW.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this User-Agent and request pattern suggest automated scanning?
benign_exceptions: Known monitoring agents (Pingdom, Datadog, etc.) in allow list
CI/CD pipeline health checks
Internal vulnerability scanner IPs in allow_listed_ips

classes.name: CVE / CMS Probe
classes.retrieval_strategy: Search for well-known CVE and CMS-specific path patterns (wp-admin, wp-login, .git, .env, phpinfo, actuator, manager/html). Any of these paths returning HTTP 200 regardless of source IP indicates a serious finding. New IPs accessing any of these paths force REVIEW.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Do these path accesses suggest CVE exploitation probing or CMS/admin panel discovery?
benign_exceptions: Penetration testing engagements from pre-approved IP ranges
Internal vulnerability scanning on allow_listed_ips

classes.name: Protocol Abuse
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate usage of unusual HTTP methods (TRACE, CONNECT, DELETE, PATCH). New source IPs using unusual methods or any IP using more than 5 distinct HTTP methods in the window suggest scanner automation or protocol abuse for reconnaissance.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this HTTP method usage pattern suggest protocol abuse or scanner activity?
benign_exceptions: REST API endpoints legitimately using DELETE/PATCH for CRUD operations
Known API clients registered in entity registry

classes.name: Exfiltration Signals
classes.retrieval_strategy: Aggregate source IPs by request count, then sum bytes_sent per top IP in 1-hour window. Outbound byte volume exceeding threshold (default 50MB/hr per IP) suggests data exfiltration. Correlate with Hmailer stream for email exfil confirmation.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this outbound byte volume suggest data exfiltration?
benign_exceptions: Known backup or reporting jobs with large legitimate downloads
CDN origin pull IPs in allow list
Software distribution endpoints with large file downloads

classes.name: Beaconing / C2 Communication
classes.retrieval_strategy: Check for the same (source IP + URI) pair appearing consistently across 3 or more consecutive hourly windows. Periodic consistent access to the same endpoint from the same IP with regular timing intervals suggests C2 beaconing.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does this IP+URI pair's periodic consistent access suggest C2 beaconing?
benign_exceptions: Monitoring agents polling status endpoints
Scheduled batch jobs with regular API calls
Known integration connectors in entity registry

classes.name: First-Occurrence Catch-All
classes.retrieval_strategy: Identify source IPs that first appeared within the last 48 hours and are still active in the current window. New entities not matching existing detection classes but showing activity warrant closer inspection for behaviors not yet covered by Classes 1-14.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Is there anything suspicious about this newly-seen entity that does not fit existing classes?
benign_exceptions: Known partner IP ranges being first seen after allow list update
Internal IPs from new infrastructure deployments
Developer workstations from new office locations

classes.name: Structural Anomaly / Obfuscation
classes.retrieval_strategy: Filter for extreme URI query string lengths (>500 chars) which may contain encoded payloads or obfuscated exploit strings. Also flag IPs using more than 5 distinct HTTP methods in the window as this suggests automated scanning with method fuzzing.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Does extreme query string length or multi-method usage suggest encoding obfuscation?
benign_exceptions: GraphQL endpoints with complex nested queries
Known rich API clients with large serialized payloads
Search endpoints accepting complex filter expressions

classes.name: AI Open Hunt
classes.retrieval_strategy: GATED: Only executes when Classes 1-16 produced at least one REVIEW or higher finding. Collects up to 20 rows with highest anomaly indicators from the current run. AI reviews for patterns not captured by existing classes. Patterns recurring 3+ times across runs trigger K-phase rule growth and addition to iis-lock-rules.json.
classes.ai_prompt_hint: Are there patterns in these anomalous rows that suggest TTPs not covered by Classes 1-16? If so, describe the pattern, proposed class name, and MITRE technique.
benign_exceptions: Patterns already covered by Classes 1-16
Noise from known scanning IPs in allow list
Application-specific behaviors documented in baseline
